title: cordia decandra
intro: Cordia decandra, also known as the Wild Jasmine or Manjari, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It is traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids in its leaves, bark, and roots. The health benefits of Cordia decandra include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Cordia decandra can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
